name: Open Door Counseling Center Dba Open Door Housingworks
description_short: Non-profit housing counseling and supportive services provider serving Washington County, OR since 1984. Offers HUD-aligned homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and homeless support services.
description_long:
Open Door HousingWorks has operated as a community-based non-profit since 1984, focusing on housing stability and financial empowerment in Washington County, Oregon and surrounding areas. The organization is structured around two primary service divisions: homeownership services and supportive housing services, reflecting a comprehensive approach to housing insecurity across income and housing-status spectrums.

The homeownership division provides HUD-aligned services including the "Make Your Move" homebuyer education workshops (offered monthly), one-on-one home buyer counseling, foreclosure prevention assistance, and financial counseling. They also administer an Individual Development Account (IDA) savings program and Down Payment Assistance (DPA) lending to help qualified borrowers access homeownership. The supportive housing division serves homeless and housing-insecure populations through street outreach, a daytime access center, emergency shelter, housing case management, and a supportive employment program.

Open Door distinguishes itself by integrating basic needs services with skill-building and mental health support. Their daytime access center provides showers, hygiene products, meals, phone/mailbox access, and a warm day room alongside case management. Case managers work directly with clients on resume building, interview skills, computer literacy, and connecting to mental health and addiction counseling—addressing root causes of homelessness rather than symptoms alone. All services are provided free to eligible clients, with no commercial lending or credit-product sales.

As a legitimate non-profit operating continuously since 1984 with published workshop schedules and clear service offerings, Open Door functions as a genuine community resource. However, their homeownership counseling—while aligned with HUD standards—is limited to education and basic counseling rather than ongoing portfolio management or credit repair. The organization's capacity appears modest (single location, business hours Monday-Friday 8am-4pm), which may create accessibility barriers for working individuals.
pros: HUD-aligned homebuyer education with monthly "Make Your Move" workshops offered in-person over full days (9am-5pm)
Integrated case management addressing multiple barriers to housing stability: employment skills, mental health, addiction, and basic needs simultaneously
Free financial counseling and foreclosure prevention services at no cost to eligible clients
Individual Development Account (IDA) savings program and Down Payment Assistance (DPA) lending to support homeownership access
Comprehensive supportive housing services including street outreach, emergency shelter, employment support, and hygiene facilities
Operating continuously since 1984 with established community partnerships and government-linked resources
All services genuinely free; no hidden fees, credit products, or commercial lending involved
cons: Limited service hours (Monday-Friday 8am-4pm only) may exclude working individuals and those with inflexible schedules
Single physical location in Hillsboro, OR limits accessibility for surrounding areas despite serving "Washington County and surrounding areas"
Homeownership services appear limited to education and basic counseling; no ongoing credit repair, dispute services, or portfolio management mentioned
No evening, weekend, or remote counseling options documented; daytime access center implies in-person-only service model
Website does not specify income limits, eligibility criteria, or typical wait times for services
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Washington County seeking HUD-aligned pre-purchase education and down payment assistance
Homeowners facing foreclosure needing prevention counseling and financial restructuring guidance
Homeless or housing-insecure individuals needing integrated case management, basic needs support, and employment skills training
Low-to-moderate income households building savings and financial stability through matched savings accounts (IDA)
diagnosis: Open Door HousingWorks is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families seeking legitimate, free housing counseling and support in Washington County, OR—whether pursuing first-time homeownership, preventing foreclosure, or stabilizing from homelessness. The primary caveat is that services are limited to education and case management; they do not offer credit repair, dispute services, or credit-building products, and their Monday-Friday daytime-only hours may exclude working individuals.
services: Home Buyer Education Workshop ("Make Your Move") — monthly full-day sessions
Daytime Access Center with hygiene facilities, meals, and day room
Housing Case Management with employment and skills support
Mental Health and Addiction Counseling Referrals
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 34420 Sw Tualatin Valley Hwy, Hillsboro, OR 97123
